world_date: Month of the Ashen Dreaming, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord, Day 21
major_event: The twenty-first day breaks with pressure on every front. In Lostfeld the case produced a fourth etching overnight — a line connecting the three spirals. The Seer has departed Stonebreak bound for Lostfeld, but the journey is three days by mountain road. Lady Orryn gives her answer in Twin Cities before noon. In Tidefall the Admiral's dawn deadline has arrived. The world does not pause.

primary_event.name: The Seer Departs Stonebreak — A Warning Travels Toward Lostfeld
primary_event.summary: The Seer rose before dawn in Stonebreak, having received a vision-pressure he could not name but could not ignore — a woman's hands on cold iron, a bloodline marked by something older than curse. He interpreted this as a command to find Kessa Stonebraid and deliver a warning: that what she tends, she should cease to tend. He does not know exactly why. He packed light and joined a Monastery supply cart heading north toward the mountain road. He will not arrive in Lostfeld for three days. The case continues to etch in his absence.

supporting_events.name: Lady Orryn Answers — The Proclamation Hinges
supporting_events.summary: Lady Orryn arrived at Caeris's office at mid-morning and gave her answer in person rather than by letter: she would sign, but only if a clause was added stipulating that her name could be withdrawn within ten days if the king recovered. Caeris had thirty minutes to decide. He agreed. The amended text must now go back to the High Cleric for re-review — adding approximately twelve hours to the timeline. The proclamation is alive but delayed.

supporting_events.name: The Admiral's Dawn Deadline — The Door Opens
supporting_events.summary: At dawn, the incapacitation protocol deadline passed. The council chair arrived at the admiralty hall with two witnesses at first light — and found the door already open. The Admiral sat at his desk, dressed, with the two unread documents open in front of him. He had read them. He looked up and said only: 'Sit.' The council chair sat. What passed between them in the next hour was heard by no one else. The chair emerged alone and called an emergency closed session.

supporting_events.name: Arkenor Receives Both Delegations — Shadow Court's Balance Point
supporting_events.summary: Lythara's envoy arrived at Arkenor's estate at dawn. Velorn's representative arrived four hours later. Arkenor received both separately, then together, and asked each side the same question: what happens to his three Gnome-pass trade routes if the other side wins. Neither delegation had prepared for that framing. Arkenor dismissed both for the day and said he would send word by evening. The Shadow Court's immediate future is in the hands of a man who thinks like a merchant.

active_events.name: Varek and the Iron Case — The Fourth Etching
active_events.summary: Overnight a fourth etching appeared on the case — a connecting line between the three spirals, forming an incomplete geometric figure. Kessa identified it at dawn and reported to Ironmaul. Varek had slept the entire night without movement for the first time since arriving — the garrison found this more unsettling than his usual sounds. Ashveil has still said nothing about the Concordance. The Seer is traveling toward them but is three days away.
active_events.consequences: If the geometric figure completes, it may constitute a readable symbol from the Concordance. Kessa is the primary custodian of the case and the entity appears to be responding to her rune-presence. The Seer's warning — if it ever arrives — may be three days too late. Ashveil's civilian courier is one day into a four-day journey to Stonebreak.

active_events.name: The Admiral Reads the Documents — Tidefall's Crisis Point
active_events.summary: The Admiral was found at his desk with both documents open at dawn — he had read them independently before the council arrived. The council chair is in a closed session. What the Admiral concluded from the Dur Khadur dispatch and the Hollow Accord letter is unknown to the council majority. The Vampire operative cannot get information from inside the closed session. The prophet has not been informed.
active_events.consequences: The Admiral's response to the Hollow Accord and the Dur Khadur contract will define Tidefall's posture for weeks. If he accepts Caeris's authority he may issue the stand-down himself — undercutting the council's protocol process. If he rejects it, the Dur Khadur situation escalates. The council is fractured and the chair may not represent majority opinion.

active_events.name: Rowen — The Fleet Moves Northeast
active_events.summary: Rowen spent the night attempting to identify a third map point using the navigator's sealed charts. By morning he had narrowed a candidate — a submerged ridge formation southeast of the Dreadwind archipelago, mentioned in an old harbormaster's note as a place ships avoid. He has not yet moved the fleet. The forty-eight-hour movement promise is now twenty-four hours old. Navigator Briggs left a chart scroll on Rowen's table with three routes highlighted, saying nothing.
active_events.consequences: Rowen must commit to a heading within twenty-four hours or break his promise to the officers. Each identified map point is a destination candidate. Moving toward the coastal shelf northeast of Tidefall would bring the fleet into contested waters. The southeast ridge candidate is safer but less certain.

active_events.name: The Proclamation Crisis — Orryn's Condition Extends the Clock
active_events.summary: Lady Orryn signed conditionally — a withdrawal clause within ten days if the king recovers. Caeris accepted. The amended text must return to the High Cleric for re-review, adding twelve hours. Proclamation is now expected by tomorrow morning at earliest. Lord Halveth was informed and is anxious about the delay. The Glenhaven Wayfarers are now formally inside Twin Cities territory as observers.
active_events.consequences: The twelve-hour extension creates exposure — every additional hour before announcement is an hour someone could act to prevent it. The Dur Khadur stand-down cannot be formally issued until after proclamation. Farrock's northern inquiry still sits unanswered on Caeris's desk.

active_events.name: The Recall Standoff — Dur Khadur Waits at Four Miles
active_events.summary: The Dur Khadur unit remains at four miles from the Twin Cities with a fourteen-day supply window confirmed. The unit commander received Caeris's stand-down acknowledgment and is waiting for the formal order. The Admiral has now read the dispatch from Tidefall's desk. Supply caravan arrived this morning — unit morale is stable. The standoff's resolution is tied directly to whether Caeris's proclamation goes through.
active_events.consequences: Dur Khadur is in a commercially rational holding pattern. If the proclamation fails and no authority issues a stand-down, the unit may begin to withdraw on its own terms — which would be interpreted as a political statement. If the proclamation succeeds, the stand-down follows within hours.

active_events.name: Shadow Court — Arkenor Holds Both Delegations
active_events.summary: Arkenor received both delegations and reframed the contest entirely around his trade routes. Neither side anticipated his question. He has dismissed both until evening. Lythara's commercial offer is structurally better suited to his interests, but Velorn arrived with council-record leverage that Arkenor has not yet revealed he values. The outcome tonight reshapes the Shadow Court's near-term power balance.
active_events.consequences: Arkenor may split his answer — giving each side partial commitment while extracting maximum concessions. If he commits to Lythara, Velorn loses his momentum. If he plays both, the Court enters a period of factional cold war. The Gnome pass trade routes are the real stakes, and the Gnomish Syndicate is not aware it is being negotiated over.

active_events.name: Gilgeth Test Caravan — Instability on the Bypass
active_events.summary: The test caravan reached the Rock Plains midpoint by evening. A third altercation among the opioid-dependent crew chiefs turned violent — one crew chief struck a guard with a cargo hook. The armed escort subdued the situation but one guard sustained a minor injury. The caravan master sent a relay rider back to Gilgeth with a status report. The caravan is two days from Frostvale.
active_events.consequences: If violence escalates before Frostvale, the bypass test fails and Gilgeth loses face with Groth. If it reaches Frostvale intact, the route is proven but the addiction problem is publicly exposed to a non-Gilgeth audience. The elder council must decide whether to recall or press forward.

active_events.name: Vilefin Tinker — Northbound Through the Rock Plains
active_events.summary: The tinker departed Farrock at dawn, northbound toward the Gnome pass. He carries his coded note to Skrix and is calculating whether the Stonebreak route is commercially viable given the Monastery situation. He passed through the Rock Plains outer edge by midday and noted fresh orc caravan tracks — the Gilgeth test run had passed through the night before. He filed this in his memory as potentially valuable.
active_events.consequences: The tinker is a mobile intelligence asset heading toward the most information-dense chokepoint in the region. If he reaches the Gnome pass station he may encounter the Seer's departure trace — the Gnomes logged his movement. His Stonebreak calculation depends on what he finds at the pass.

faction_actions.action: Departed Stonebreak at dawn on a Monastery supply cart heading north toward the mountain road to Lostfeld. He carries the god-vision as a felt imperative: a woman and cold iron and a bloodline marked. He is interpreting it as a warning to Kessa Stonebraid to cease her work with the case. He has not framed this as a curse — he is uncertain of that word — and will likely deliver it as 'what you hold may hold you in return.'
faction_actions.reason: The Seer interprets divine pressure as physical obligation. He cannot send a message through intermediaries for something this specific — he must go. He has three days of mountain road ahead.
faction_actions.target: Kessa Stonebraid — primary destination; Lostfeld garrison — arrival in approximately 3 days; Stonebreak — departed, no longer present

faction_actions.action: Ironmaul called a full Thane's Council session by courier — summoning the two nearest council members to the garrison within two days. He is not yet revealing the entity or the etchings in the summons text; he framed it as an 'institutional matter requiring in-person consultation.' He ordered Kessa to continue her morning rune-checks and document every change. He still has not asked Ashveil directly about the marks.
faction_actions.reason: Ironmaul is building a formal institutional response before revealing how serious the situation is. He wants council authority behind him before disclosing the entity. His intelligence tier means he is acting deliberately but is not yet connecting the Concordance significance.
faction_actions.target: Thane's Council — two members summoned, arriving in two days; Kessa — ordered to continue documentation; Ashveil — watched but not directly confronted

faction_actions.action: Ashveil continued her morning observation of the case and noted the fourth etching in silence. She spent the afternoon writing a careful account of the Concordance symbol progression — not to share with Ironmaul, but to encode into a memory-binding she will carry on her person in case she is separated from her letters. She also sent a brief verbal message to Varek, approaching him in the yard: 'What does the figure want to complete?' Varek did not answer with words — he drew a circle in the dirt with his finger.
faction_actions.reason: Ashveil is documenting everything as personal insurance and trying to extract information from Varek through indirect questioning. The circle response suggests the geometric figure on the case has a completion point — a full closed form.
faction_actions.target: Varek — indirect questioning, partial response obtained; case progression — documented in personal memory-binding; Ironmaul — still not informed of Concordance significance

faction_actions.action: Caeris returned the amended proclamation text to the High Cleric's office by midday with a note asking for expedited review given the conditional clause change. He sent a brief holding message to Lord Halveth. He also finally read Farrock's inquiry about the northern corridor and composed a careful reply: he confirmed that unusual northern activity had been noted and that the regent-designate's office was monitoring the situation. He did not mention Lostfeld or Varek — he does not know those details.
faction_actions.reason: Caeris is managing four simultaneous threads. The Farrock reply is politically important — ignoring it would create an enemy at a sensitive moment. His answer is deliberately vague because his actual knowledge is vague.
faction_actions.target: High Cleric — amended text submitted, expedited review requested; Lord Halveth — brief holding message sent; Farrock — northern corridor reply dispatched

faction_actions.action: The council chair emerged from the closed session with the Admiral at midday and called an emergency closed council meeting. No statement was issued publicly. The Vampire operative confirmed the session occurred but could not obtain content. The prophet was seen waiting outside the admiralty hall for three hours and then departing without being received. The Dur Khadur dispatch and Hollow Accord letter are now presumably known to the Admiral.
faction_actions.reason: The council is managing a situation they do not fully control — the Admiral's return to visibility changes the power calculus and the closed session suggests either negotiation or confrontation, not resolution.
faction_actions.target: Emergency council session — called and ongoing; Admiral — present, communicating; prophet — excluded; public — no statement issued

faction_actions.action: Lythara, having received no word from her envoy yet, spent the day reviewing the Vampire operative's grey-cloaked courier report. The physical description — tall, grey cloak, no weapons, harbor inn, three days before the Admiral sealed his study — matched no known Shadow Court asset. She instructed the operative to cross-reference the description against Tidefall's known independent broker network and against any Outerlands-adjacent contact records.
faction_actions.reason: Lythara is methodical. The ruling-out of institutional origin means the actor is either a private party or something operating outside her intelligence categories. She is treating this as a threat until proven otherwise.
faction_actions.target: Vampire operative — cross-reference order issued; grey-cloaked figure — active investigation; Outerlands contacts — being checked

faction_actions.action: The Dark Council received a relay message that the Admiral had read their dispatch. They convened a brief session and concluded: wait. They do not act until a formal authority issues a stand-down or a conflict begins. The resupply caravan reached the unit camp. They sent no further messages to any party today.
faction_actions.reason: Dur Khadur's commercial rationality means they will not move unnecessarily. Both Caeris and the Admiral have now received their communication — they are waiting for a response that produces a clear contractual pathway.
faction_actions.target: Unit camp — resupplied, holding; Dark Council — in observation mode; Caeris and Admiral — both awaiting formal response

faction_actions.action: The two Wayfarers formally entered Twin Cities territory and took up observer positions near the regent-designate's office district. They send daily encoded reports to Elowen. Elowen received a brief reply from the Gnome pass station about route conditions — the station noted only that 'traffic was unusual this week' without elaborating. She filed this against her earlier Lostfeld concern.
faction_actions.reason: Elowen is threading multiple monitoring operations simultaneously. The Gnome pass reply is not alarming but it is suggestive — she now suspects the Lostfeld situation is more active than she knew.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities — two Wayfarers embedded as observers; Gnome pass — unusual traffic noted, filed; Lostfeld concern — elevated from suspicion to active monitoring

faction_actions.action: The pass station logged the Seer's departure northward on the Monastery supply cart and noted it in the daily movement record. The station keeper sent the Seer off with a supply of preserved travel food — a customary courtesy — and asked no questions. Internally, the syndicate council discussed the old Tidefall cipher anomaly from the anchorage request. They decided to offer the information for sale but only to parties who could demonstrate they already knew part of the story.
faction_actions.reason: The Gnomes never give intelligence away. The cipher anomaly is worth money only to someone already investigating Tidefall's admiralty network. Selling it blind would reduce its value.
faction_actions.target: Seer departure — logged; Tidefall cipher intelligence — prepared for conditional sale; anchorage request record — retained as asset

faction_actions.action: Rowen studied the navigator's routes overnight and made his decision before dawn: northeast, toward the coastal shelf he identified as the second map point. He called the officers at first light and gave the order — they weigh anchor at midday. He told the navigator the heading but not the destination's significance. The fleet began preparation. Briggs watched Rowen's face as the order was given and said nothing, but filed the observation.
faction_actions.reason: Rowen has calculated that staying costs more than moving. The northeastern heading takes him toward the second map point and keeps him away from the contested Dreadwind archipelago where his enemies hold ports. Movement maintains morale and advances the map.
faction_actions.target: Fleet — underway at midday, northeast heading; navigator Briggs — given heading, not significance; second map point — primary destination, 2-3 days sailing

faction_actions.action: The operative spent the day cross-referencing the grey-cloaked figure against Tidefall's independent broker network. By evening they had eliminated twelve known brokers by physical description. What remained was a shorter list of three possible matches — all of whom had connections to the pre-current-Admiral trading network that operated during the previous Admiral's tenure, five to eight years ago. The operative sent Lythara an updated report: 'The actor appears connected to the old admiralty network, not current power. Motive unclear — possibly old agenda resurfacing.'
faction_actions.reason: The Vampires are systematic investigators. The narrowing suggests the grey-cloaked figure is not a current power player but someone from the previous regime acting on an older agenda — which is potentially more dangerous than a current rival.
faction_actions.target: Grey-cloaked figure — narrowed to three candidates from old admiralty network; Lythara — updated report transmitted; old admiralty network — now active thread of investigation

faction_actions.action: The garrison commander received Caeris's vague northern corridor reply by afternoon. He read it twice, noted that it confirmed awareness but gave no specifics, and concluded: Caeris knows something but is managing it at the political level, not the military one. He ordered the northern watchtower crew to extend their signal frequency to every three hours and drafted a brief note to the Thane's Council at Lostfeld — not through official channels, but through a garrison-to-garrison courier that uses the old joint-patrol protocol from thirty years ago.
faction_actions.reason: Farrock's garrison commander is old-fashioned and direct. If Caeris won't give specifics, he'll go sideways — garrison to garrison. The old joint-patrol courier is slower but harder to intercept.
faction_actions.target: Northern watchtowers — signal every three hours; Thane's Council at Lostfeld — informal garrison courier dispatched; Caeris reply — noted as politically managed, specifics lacking

faction_actions.action: The elder council received the relay rider's report of the violent altercation. After a heated session, they voted three to two to press forward — withdrawing now would be interpreted by Groth as proof that Gilgeth cannot control its own caravans. They dispatched a second relay rider with orders to the caravan master: press to Frostvale, double the escort watch on the three most unstable crew chiefs, and under no circumstances allow another incident to reach Frostvale's gates before the caravan does.
faction_actions.reason: Gilgeth is trapped between saving face and managing a real addiction-driven instability problem. The elder council's three-to-two vote reflects genuine internal fracture. The decision to press forward is an act of political stubbornness as much as strategic planning.
faction_actions.target: Test caravan — ordered to continue; three unstable crew chiefs — under doubled escort watch; Frostvale — caravan expected in two days; Groth — image management driving decision

faction_actions.action: The tinker reached the Rock Plains outer edge and identified Gilgeth test caravan tracks. He detoured slightly to follow the tracks for half a mile, gathering what information he could from the road signs — cargo weight, number of animals, pace. He concluded a large Gilgeth caravan had passed the night before on an unusual route. He added this to his mental inventory and continued north. Skrix's coded reply reached Farrock's gate outbox where the tinker had left his note — but the tinker was already gone.
faction_actions.reason: The tinker is building a picture. The Gilgeth caravan on an unusual route is worth noting. He won't sell it immediately — he'll hold it until the right buyer appears.
faction_actions.target: Gilgeth caravan tracks — identified and studied; Skrix reply — missed, still at Farrock gate; Gnome pass — continuing north, estimated arrival tomorrow

recent_events.text: The Seer departed Stonebreak before dawn on a Monastery supply cart. He carries a warning he has interpreted as: 'what you hold may hold you in return' — directed at Kessa Stonebraid. He is three days from Lostfeld by mountain road. The case continues to etch in his absence.

A fourth etching appeared overnight — a line connecting the three spirals into an incomplete geometric figure. Varek slept without movement for the first time, which the garrison found more unsettling than his usual sounds. Ashveil drew an indirect answer from Varek: a circle drawn in dirt, suggesting the figure on the case wants to close itself. Kessa continues her morning rune-checks as ordered.

Lady Orryn signed conditionally — a ten-day withdrawal clause if the king recovers. Caeris accepted and returned the amended text to the High Cleric for re-review. Proclamation is delayed by approximately twelve hours, now expected tomorrow morning. Caeris sent a vague but confirmatory reply to Farrock's northern inquiry. Glenhaven Wayfarers are now embedded in the city as observers.

The Admiral was found at dawn with both unread documents open on his desk. He had read them. He received the council chair, said 'Sit,' and closed the door. One hour later the chair called an emergency closed council session. No public statement was issued. The prophet waited outside for three hours and was not received.

Lythara's envoy arrived at dawn; Velorn's representative four hours later. Arkenor received both and asked each the same question: what happens to his three Gnome-pass trade routes if the other side wins. Neither side had an answer. He dismissed both until evening. The Shadow Court's near future waits on a merchant's calculation.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld — The Case Completes Itself While the Seer Travels
active_tensions.description: The geometric figure on the case is building toward a closed form — Varek's circle-in-dirt confirms it. Kessa is the primary custodian and the entity appears to be responding to her rune-presence. The Seer's warning is three days away. Ashveil knows what the figure means and is choosing silence. Ironmaul is summoning council members who will arrive in two days — into a situation that may have already changed.

active_tensions.factions: Twin Cities — Proclamation Delayed, Exposure Window Opens
active_tensions.description: Orryn's conditional signature extended the clock by twelve hours. Every additional hour before announcement is an hour for interference. The Dur Khadur stand-down, the Farrock northern inquiry, and the Glenhaven embedding are all now active simultaneously — Caeris is managing more threads than one person can safely hold.

active_tensions.factions: Tidefall — Admiral Returns, Council Fractures Further
active_tensions.description: The Admiral read both documents and is now in a closed session with the council chair. The council majority doesn't know what was said. The prophet was excluded. The Vampire operative has no intelligence from inside the session. Whatever the Admiral concludes will reshape Tidefall's relationship with the Hollow Accord and the Dur Khadur standoff simultaneously.

active_tensions.factions: Shadow Court — Arkenor's Answer Coming at Evening
active_tensions.description: Both delegations wait. Arkenor has reframed the contest around his Gnome-pass trade routes — a question neither Lythara nor Velorn prepared for. His answer by evening determines which faction holds Shadow Court's near-term direction. The Gnomish Syndicate is unaware it is being negotiated over.

seer_state.belief_about_user: The presence beyond the veil is not fully understood.

seer_journey.purpose: The Seer Departs Stonebreak — A Warning Travels Toward Lostfeld
seer_journey.last_outcome: The Seer rose before dawn in Stonebreak, having received a vision-pressure he could not name but could not ignore — a woman's hands on cold iron, a bloodline marked by something older than curse. He interpreted this as a command to find Kessa Stonebraid and deliver a warning: that what she tends, she should cease to tend. He does not know exactly why. He packed light and joined a Monastery supply cart heading north toward the mountain road. He will not arrive in Lostfeld for three days. The case continues to etch in his absence.
